caption: The video appears to be an educational or informational presentation discussing the dynamics of a rural community food system, specifically focusing on retailers willing to source local products and how it impacts product cost and price. The presenter, Gene Bellinger, is seen in a small window on the right side of the screen, wearing headphones and speaking about the topic. The main content of the video is displayed on the left side, featuring a diagram that illustrates the relationship between retailers sourcing local products, availability, product cost, and local consumption. The text explains that as retailers increase the availability of locally sourced products, the product cost tends to lower due to supply and demand principles, which in turn increases the likelihood of local consumption. This cycle supports the retailers who are willing to source local food, creating a reinforcing growth pattern within the retail food system cluster. The diagram uses various colored arrows and labels to visually represent this cycle, with a legend explaining the symbols used.
